title: Biotechnology and Its Applications
subtitle: Insulin, Bt cotton, gene therapy, RNAi, molecular diagnosis, transgenic animals and biosafety ethics - a premium visual applications guide.

heroModel.title: Biotech applications map
heroModel.subtitle: Drag to rotate - scroll to zoom

roadmapTitle: Biotechnology applications convert genes into medicines, crops and diagnostics.
roadmapDescription: Remember the chapter by outcomes: therapeutic proteins, resistant crops, gene correction, silencing, diagnosis and ethical control.
flowchart:
flowchart LR
  A[Recombinant DNA] --> B[Insulin]
  A --> C[Gene therapy]
  A --> D[Transgenic animals]
  A --> E[GM crops]
  E --> F[Bt cotton]
  E --> G[RNA interference]
  A --> H[Molecular diagnosis]
  H --> I[ELISA and PCR]
  A --> J[Biosafety ethics]

roadmap.title: Recombinant insulin
roadmap.summary: Human insulin chains produced using recombinant microbes.

roadmap.title: Gene therapy
roadmap.summary: Correcting defective genes, with ADA deficiency as classic example.

roadmap.title: Molecular diagnosis
roadmap.summary: PCR, ELISA and probes detect disease early.

roadmap.title: Bt crops
roadmap.summary: Bt toxin protects plants from insect pests.

roadmap.title: Transgenic animals
roadmap.summary: Animals with foreign genes help study disease and products.

roadmap.title: Biosafety and GEAC
roadmap.summary: GM organisms need safety assessment and regulation.

roadmap.title: Biopiracy and patents
roadmap.summary: Ethical use and benefit sharing matter.

conceptDescription: Each card explains what was inserted, where it acts, and what benefit or concern follows.

sections.title: Genetically engineered insulin
sections.description: Recombinant DNA technology produces human insulin in microbial systems.
points: Earlier insulin from animals could cause immune reactions.
Human insulin has A and B chains.
Proinsulin includes C peptide; mature insulin lacks C peptide.
Recombinant microbes can produce insulin chains.
sections.neet: Mature insulin consists of A and B chains joined by disulphide bonds.

sections.title: Bt cotton and Cry protein
sections.description: Bt cotton carries a gene from Bacillus thuringiensis that protects against specific insects.
points: Cry protein is produced as inactive protoxin.
Alkaline insect gut activates the toxin.
Activated toxin damages midgut epithelial cells.
Bt crops reduce certain insect pest damage.
sections.neet: Protoxin becomes active in alkaline gut of insect.

sections.title: Gene therapy and ADA deficiency
sections.description: Gene therapy aims to correct a defective gene in patient cells.
points: ADA deficiency affects immune function.
Lymphocytes can be removed, corrected and returned.
Bone marrow cell correction may give longer benefit.
Somatic gene therapy is discussed at school level.
sections.neet: ADA deficiency is the standard NCERT gene therapy example.

sections.title: RNA interference
sections.description: RNAi silences gene expression by degrading target mRNA.
points: dsRNA triggers RNAi pathway.
mRNA is degraded or translation is blocked.
RNAi can protect tobacco roots against nematode.
Process is highly sequence-specific.
sections.neet: RNAi works at mRNA level, not by cutting DNA.

sections.title: Molecular diagnosis
sections.description: Molecular tests detect disease even when pathogen level is low.
points: PCR amplifies pathogen nucleic acid.
ELISA uses antigen-antibody interaction.
DNA probes bind complementary sequences.
Early diagnosis improves treatment success.
sections.neet: PCR can detect very low pathogen load by amplification.

sections.title: Transgenic animals
sections.description: Transgenic animals carry and express foreign genes for research and products.
points: They help study gene function and disease models.
They can produce biological products.
They support vaccine safety and toxicity testing.
Ethical treatment is essential.
sections.neet: Rosie cow produced human protein-enriched milk in NCERT example.

sections.title: Biosafety, GEAC and patents
sections.description: Applications require safety regulation and ethical control.
points: GEAC evaluates GM organism release in India.
Biosafety assesses environmental and health risks.
Patents protect inventions but can raise access issues.
Biopiracy is exploitation of bioresources without proper permission or benefit sharing.
sections.neet: GEAC is the approval body associated with GM organisms in India.

sections.title: Responsible biotechnology
sections.description: Useful biotechnology must be paired with safety, monitoring and public benefit.
points: GM crops need resistance-management strategies.
Therapies need clinical testing.
Diagnostics need accuracy and quality control.
Benefit sharing is important for traditional knowledge.
sections.neet: NEET often tests both benefit and ethical concern.

modelDescription: Rotate transgene, vector, therapy and product models.
highYield: Animal insulin could cause immune reactions.
Mature insulin has A and B chains.
C peptide is absent in mature insulin.
Bt gene comes from Bacillus thuringiensis.
Cry protein is inactive protoxin in plant.
Alkaline insect gut activates Bt toxin.
ADA deficiency is gene therapy example.
RNAi uses dsRNA to silence mRNA.
Nematode resistance in tobacco is RNAi example.
Rosie cow produced human protein-enriched milk.
Biopiracy exploits bioresources without fair permission.

mistakes.title: Insulin chains
mistakes.body: Mature insulin has A and B chains; C peptide is removed from proinsulin.

mistakes.title: Bt activation site
mistakes.body: Bt protoxin becomes active in alkaline insect gut, not in the plant cell.

mistakes.title: RNAi target
mistakes.body: RNAi degrades/silences mRNA; it is not the same as gene knockout at DNA level.

quiz.question: Mature human insulin contains:
quiz.explain: C peptide is removed from proinsulin.

quiz.question: Bt toxin is activated in insect gut because it is:
ADA deficiency is linked with:
quiz.explain: ADA deficiency is the classic gene therapy example.

quiz.question: ELISA is based on:
quiz.explain: ELISA detects molecules using antigen-antibody binding.

quiz.question: Rosie is an example of:
quiz.explain: Rosie cow produced human protein-rich milk.

quiz.question: Biopiracy refers to:
quiz.explain: Biopiracy involves unfair use of bioresources/traditional knowledge.

flashcards.back: Removed from proinsulin to form mature insulin.
Inactive toxin activated in alkaline insect gut.
Correct defective ADA gene in patient cells.
Indian approval body for GM organisms.

revisionTable: A and B chains, C peptide removed
Cry protoxin activated in alkaline gut
